channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: LTM Limited Proposes Record ₹53 Dividend, Highest Ever
item.description: LTM Limited, formerly LTIMindtree, has proposed a record ₹53 per share dividend for FY26, its highest ever. Pending shareholder approval, this brings the total FY26 payout to ₹75. The announcement follows strong dividends from IT peers like OFSS and Mphasis, alongside an increased FII stake.

item.title: India Pharma: Drug Safety Reports Now Start at Market Launch, Not Approval
item.description: India's drug regulator, CDSCO, now requires pharmaceutical firms to start submitting Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSURs) from the drug's actual market launch date, not its regulatory approval date. This aims to improve real-world safety data collection and patient monitoring by closing the gap between approval and public use.

item.title: Mythos AI Highlights Cyber Gaps in Indian Firms Amid Rising Threats
item.description: Advanced AI tools like Mythos are not the primary threat but expose deep-seated cybersecurity vulnerabilities in Indian enterprises. Experts warn that the gap between discovering and exploiting flaws is shrinking, while many organizations struggle with delayed patching and weak defenses, leading to a surge in sophisticated attacks.

item.title: India Clears New Labour Codes: Minimum Wage Formula Dropped, Wider Gaps Feared
item.description: The Indian government has notified final rules for all four new labour codes, set to fully implement in November 2025. Key changes include an eight-hour workday, a 48-hour weekly cap, mandatory wage slips, and social security funds for gig and unorganized workers. However, the final wage code rules omit the previous minimum wage calculation criteria, raising concerns among experts about potential lower wages and increased disparity for workers across states.

item.title: Brigade Hotel Ventures Plans ₹1,000 Crore Karnataka Hotel Expansion
item.description: Brigade Hotel Ventures announced a significant ₹1,000 crore investment over five years in Karnataka's hospitality sector, emphasizing experiential tourism and premium markets. This strategic expansion aims to add new properties and modernize existing assets, building on the success of its flagship Grand Mercure Mysuru. The move supports its broader "Vision 2031" which targets 1,700 room keys nationwide.

item.title: RFBL Flexi Pack IPO: Debt Reduction Key to Growth Push
item.description: RFBL Flexi Pack Limited is launching an Initial Public Offering (IPO) on the NSE SME platform, priced between ₹47 and ₹50 per share. A key goal is to use about ₹35 crore from the IPO to pay down existing debt, aiming to strengthen the company's finances. The IPO, opening May 12, 2026, involves 7,065,000 new shares. The company has shown strong financial growth, with revenue reaching ₹135.46 crore and profit after tax at ₹8.33 crore in fiscal year 2025.

item.title: Behavioral Finance: Why Discipline Beats Market Timing in 2026's Tough Markets
item.description: In May 2026's volatile markets, shaped by geopolitical tensions and economic shifts, investor discipline is crucial. Behavioral finance research reveals that emotional trading, herd mentality, and overconfidence worsen market swings. Long-term investing success comes from a steady, rational approach, not market timing. This disciplined strategy, often seen in passive or long-term active funds, provides a more reliable way to grow wealth.

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay Faces Wealth Scrutiny Over ₹624 Crore Assets
item.description: Actor-turned-politician Vijay is now Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister after his party TVK's strong electoral debut. His election affidavit shows assets of ₹624 crore, making him one of India's richest state leaders. This large personal fortune, mostly in liquid assets, differs greatly from what most Chief Ministers hold and sparks questions about transparency, influence, and governance in a state where film stars have often been political leaders.

item.title: China Cancer Drugs Cut India Prices, Disrupting Western Pharma
item.description: India's cancer drug market is changing fast as affordable immunotherapy from China arrives through local deals. Priced far below Western options, these drugs offer treatment to more patients and disrupt pricing for global drugmakers, creating chances for Indian firms working with Chinese innovators.

item.title: India Hospitals Expand Capacity Amid Profit Margin Squeeze
item.description: India's hospital sector is rapidly expanding capacity due to a critical bed shortage and high demand. Leading chains like Apollo Hospitals and Max Healthcare are adding beds, but high occupancy rates are challenged by a complex payer mix. Government schemes boost volume but pressure margins, pushing hospitals to focus on efficiency and high-margin services to keep growing amid rising costs and investor attention.

item.title: India's Informal Sector: Debt Shrinks, Investment Falls
item.description: India's informal businesses are cutting debt, with loans dropping 20% and interest payments down 16%, according to the 2025 Annual Survey of Unincorporated Sector Enterprises. However, this debt reduction comes as investment in new fixed assets fell 14%, suggesting businesses are hesitant to expand. This trend, alongside uneven state performance and contrasting formal credit growth for MSMEs, points to cautious economic conditions and deeper issues.

item.title: US-China Summit: Trade Hopes Tested by Tech Barriers
item.description: The upcoming US-China summit on May 14-15 is a key event for global markets. While expectations for a major breakthrough are low, any easing of trade tensions could boost Chinese stocks. Investors are watching for signs that reduce geopolitical risk and address deep-seated disagreements. The talks will cover trade tariffs, technology export controls, rare earth supply chains, and farm purchases. Despite hopes for a calmer trade relationship, existing tariffs, tech restrictions, and competition for AI leadership present significant challenges that could stir market volatility.

item.title: Indian IT's AI Services Boom vs. US Infrastructure Valuation Surge
item.description: US tech giants pour billions into AI infrastructure, fueling high valuations. Indian IT firms excel at AI services, earning billions, yet face a valuation discount. This report explores the reasons behind this gap, looking at differing roles in AI development and implementation.

item.title: 8 Indian Stocks Earn Brokerage Buy Ratings Amid Market Caution
item.description: Indian brokerages are backing eight key stocks with 'Buy' ratings, highlighting strong performance, market gains, and clear earnings. These picks cover sectors from auto to IT and digital payments, with analysts predicting substantial gains. Still, investors need to watch competition, margins, and sector hurdles to see if the optimism holds.

item.title: India's Fuel Price Freeze: Billions Lost, Rupee Under Pressure
item.description: Despite a 36% surge in crude oil prices due to the West Asia conflict, India has maintained static petrol prices. This intervention, while politically popular, creates significant economic challenges. State-owned refineries face billions in monthly losses, the current account deficit is projected to widen, and the rupee faces sustained downward pressure. The strategy highlights India's vulnerability as a major energy importer, unlike nations with domestic production.

item.title: Indian Investors Pour ₹2.5 Lakh Crore Into Stocks, Backing Domestic Growth
item.description: In the January-March 2026 quarter, Indian institutional investors (DIIs) injected ₹2.5 lakh crore into Indian equities, showing strong confidence despite market turbulence. Mutual funds were key drivers, offsetting selling by foreign investors (FIIs) and retail traders. DIIs increased stakes in companies with strong domestic demand, digital capabilities, and expanding operations, focusing on housing finance, fintech, IT services, and retail.

item.title: Kela's ₹177 Crore Bet: Contrarian Buys 4 Stocks Targeting Turnarounds
item.description: Veteran investor Madhusudan Kela invested ₹177 crore in four companies in the March 2026 quarter. His strategy targets contrarian bets and opportunities missed by most investors, including Aptech Ltd, Indiabulls Ltd, Simplex Infrastructures Ltd, and Subam Papers Ltd, all chosen for their turnaround or growth potential.

item.title: Mahindra Dominates India's Diesel SUVs as BS7 Costs Rise
item.description: Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) now leads India's diesel passenger vehicle market, taking over half the share mainly thanks to its popular SUVs. But new BS7 emission standards are coming, which will make diesel cars much more expensive to build. This could change the diesel market significantly. M&M is already working on electric vehicles (EVs) and other engine types, as its strong position in diesel faces pressure from regulations and buyers wanting cleaner options.
